commissionFee: Graduate & UG = $1200 for each term during the first 4 consecutive semesters.  For Online Graduate or UG degree program, Commission = $100 per credit hour or $300 per course upto 12 credit hours during the first 4 consecutive semesters. IEI = 10% of fees paid for the first 4 sessions. If the student receives direct financial assistance from the University to pay for tuition and fees in the form of a scholarship or assistantship, the related commission rate shall be adjusted to the 10% of the net tuition and mandatory fees paid by the student for the said semester. The University shall not be financially obligated to pay the vendor any of the commissions noted above if the student receives full financial assistance for tuition and fees for any semester. No Commission for Summer Enrolment.  Any undergraduate student enrolled in less than 12 credit hours or graduate student enrolled in less than 9 credit hours is considered as a part-time student. For part time UG/Graduate program, Commission = $500 for the first 4 consecutive semesters.

name: KWANTLEN POLYTECHNIC UNIVERSITY (KPU)
commissionFee:
Commission = 15% of two semesters, 
if a student pays fees for 9 or more credit hours. If a student enrols for Pathway courses for 2 semesters, KPU will pay for those 2 semesters only (30 credit hours). 
No Follow-on commission
W.e.f. Fall 2025, Commission = 15% of first two semesters, if a student pays fees for 9 or more credit hours.                                     If a student pays Tuition Fees for less than nine (9) credit hours in a semester, commission= 15% of the Tuition Fees for the actual number of credit hours paid for such semester. If a student is upgrading to an Undergraduate (UG) program upon successful completion of ACP courses, the Agent shall be entitled to receive commission for the first two semesters of the pathway program, regardless of the pathway level at which the student is enrolled. Upon the student's successful admission to a UG program and enrollment in a minimum of three (3) credit-bearing courses, the Agent shall be entitled to an additional commission.
The maximum amount of commission payable to the Agent for any given student is 15% of the Tuition Fees for 30 credit hours, regardless of the number of credit hours of study such student is enrolled in at KPU during the first
two semesters of a student's First Year of Study.

name: AUT University & AUT INTERNATIONAL HOUSE - AGENCY CODE: AUT0078
programs:
AUT International will pay follow-on commission to our agents who send students to multiple programmes within the university. 
1.        This includes the following pathways within AUT: 
1.        Certificate into Bachelor 
2.        Diploma into Bachelor 
3.        Bachelor into Honours 
4.        Bachelor into Graduate Diploma 
5.        Graduate Diploma into Post-Graduate Diploma or Masters 
6.        Bachelor or Honours into Post-Graduate Diploma or Masters 
7.        Post Graduate Diploma into Masters 
2.        This excludes the following pathways within AUT: 
1.        IH courses into mainstream AUT study (follow on commission already in place for this) 
3.        Certificates into Diplomas
4.        Any post-graduate programme into PhD 
3.        This change runs for the 2021 academic year only (including any returning student who starts their second programme at AUT in any 2021 intake). Note – follow-on commission does not apply if a student defers their second programme to start in 2022 (and is therefore not a returning student in 2021)
A note around this incentive, please be aware of our Change and Appoint an Agent Policy: 
•        An agent may encourage their existing student to apply for a second course of study with no time lapsed between the student’s first and second programme at AUT. In this case the agent will receive follow-on commission. 
•        AUT will not allow a student to change their agent to apply for a second course of study unless at least 1 year has lapsed between the completion of their first programme and the start of a new programme. 
•        A student who applied directly to AUT for their first programme, may appoint an agent to support their application for a second programme if they do not yet have a full Offer of Place for their new programme.

September 2025:                                                                                                                                              A.1 REN will remunerate The Agent based on the student recruitments and enrollments at Raffles College of Higher Education for the courses listed below:
a. 1-year Master of Arts courses.
b. 1-year Bachelor of Arts with Honours (Top Up) courses.
c. 6-month Diploma and 18 month Advanced Diploma courses.
d. 1-year IGCSE course.
e. 3-month Foundation Studies Certificate course.
f. 3–6-month English Language courses.
A.2 Agent commissions payable for each student recruited in a term are as follows:
a. 1st PAYMENT: 10% of first year course fees for 2-year programmes (under 6-month Diploma + 18-month Advanced Diploma).
b. 2nd PAYMENT: 5% of total course fees for 1-year Bachelor of Arts with Honours (Top Up) courses.
c. 3rd PAYMENT: 5% of total course fees for 1-year Master of Arts course.
A.3 Agent commissions payable for each student recruited in a term, for credit transfer applicant, who is granted exemption for Diploma and/or Advanced Diploma courses, are as follows: a. 1st PAYMENT: 5% of total course fees for Diploma and Advanced Diploma courses.
b. 2nd PAYMENT: 5% of total course fees for 1-year Bachelor of Arts with Honours (Top Up) courses.
A.4 If a student only applies for Diploma + Advanced Diploma courses, payable agent commission is up to Advanced Diploma course. a. There is no additional agent commission payable if the student decides to continue for Bachelor of Arts with Honours (Top Up) course.
Agent commission at 5% of the total course fees is payable for each student recruited in a term only for any of the following programmes:
a. 1-year Master of Arts.
b. 1-year Bachelor of Arts with Honours (Top Up).
c. 6-month Diploma.
d. 18-month Advanced Diploma.
e. Bridging Courses for credit transfer students under Diploma and/or Advanced Diploma.
f. 1-year IGCSE.
i. If a student only applies for IGCSE, payable agent commission is only for IGCSE.
ii. There are no additional agent commissions payable if the student decides to continue for Diploma, Advanced Diploma and Bachelor of Arts with Honours (Top Up).
g. 3-month Foundation Studies Certificate.
h. English Language courses (Foundation English, Advanced English, Higher English Language Programme-HELP).
